text: The surgeon performs a pancreato-jejunostomy using barbed sutures, creating a lateral terminal anastomosis. The posterior wall of the pancreato-jejunal anastomosis is completed laparoscopically, allowing visualization and manipulation of the Virsung canals. An entherotomy is made in the jejunum using a monopolar bisturi, followed by the passage of the drenthutter. The anterior wall is then sutured in the same manner, securing the Virsung canals to the jejunum.

The surgeon completes the suture using resorbable barbed wire, marked as three zeros, to telescope the regional preference onto the distal pancreas. The suture is visually finished, with threads that do not require tying at the end.

The surgeon tightens the sutures independently, completing the posterior suture line. An adequate enterotomy is made to insert the lost drain into the jejunum, initiating the second suture line with self-locking barbed wires in a continuous fashion. The surgical specimen is then extruded through a phallostil wound.
